Vol. 16 | No. 3 |1892-1905| July - September | 2023 [substituted-1H-pyrazol- 4-yl] has a structure–activity connection. In contrast to the benzothiazole moiety (18e and 18f) in target compounds, novel compounds comprising benzimidazole (18b, 18c, 18g, 18h, 18i, 18j, and 18k) and benzoxazole (18a and 18d) demonstrated broad-spectrum antibacterial profiles against tested species. No effect of electron-donating and withdrawing groups in target compounds (18a– 18k) is observed on the bactericidal action against the tested bacterial strain.47 X= O, R= H , R = 2-OH ( 18a), X= NH , R= H , R= 2-OH (18b) N R 1 1 X N X= NH , R= H , R= 2-OH (18c) , X= O, R= H, R= 2-OH (18d) 1 1 N X2 X2 = NH2 , O, S X= S, R= H , R= 2-OH ( 18e) , X= S, R= H, R= H (18f) 1 1 18a- 18k X= NH, R= H, R=H (18g) , X= NH, R= NO, R= H ( 18h) R 1 1 2 1 R1 = EWG or WDG X= NH, R= NO, R=H (18i) , X= NH, R= NO, R= 2-OH (18j) Activity = unaffected 1 2 1 2 X= NH, R= NO, R= 2-OH (18k) X2 = NH2 , O, broad-spectrum antibacterial profiles 1 2 X2 = S, do not have broad-spectrum antibacterial profiles Fig.-9: Benzothiazoles, Benzoxazoles, and Benzimidazole hybrids Novel 1, 3, 5-trisubstituted Pyrazoline Hybridized with Benzimidazole...

Vol. 16 | No. 3 |1892-1905| July - September | 2023 Antibacterial Activity of New Quinazoline-Benzimidazole Hybrid Derivatives Quinazoline is a unique moiety with remarkable activities such as antitubercular, antibacterial (Fig.-16)71, anticonvulsant72, antifungal73, anti-inflammatory74, and anti-tumor.75 4(3H)-quinazolinones have been identified as an orally accessible antibacterial agent by Bouley et al.76Antibacterial and antimycobacterial properties of 15 novel quinazoline benzimidazole synthesized hybrids were assessed. Findings indicated that the synthesized compounds had S. aureus-specific inhibitory action. With a MIC of 4µg/mL, Derivatives 47b, 47c, 47h, and 47i (Fig.-17) showed similar antibacterial action. Derivatives 47g, 48a, 48b, and 49 showed considerable potency with MICs of 16 to 64µg/mL. The alteration in -COOH group at the C-4 phenyl (at the para position) by coupling with modified benzimidazoloamines to different amides as a result, the derivatives 48a, 48b, 48c, and 48d have been created.With MIC (32- 64 µg/mL), the synthesized compounds displayed mild suppression against S. aureus. At C-4, 2-trifluoromethyl benzimidazolo-5-amine was substituted for 2-trifluoro...

Vol. 16 | No. 3 |1892-1905| July - September | 2023 Antibacterial Activity of New Quinazoline-Benzimidazole Hybrid Derivatives Quinazoline is a unique moiety with remarkable activities such as antitubercular, antibacterial (Fig.-16)71, anticonvulsant72, antifungal73, anti-inflammatory74, and anti-tumor.75 4(3H)-quinazolinones have been identified as an orally accessible antibacterial agent by Bouley et al.76Antibacterial and antimycobacterial properties of 15 novel quinazoline benzimidazole synthesized hybrids were assessed. Findings indicated that the synthesized compounds had S. aureus-specific inhibitory action. With a MIC of 4µg/mL, Derivatives 47b, 47c, 47h, and 47i (Fig.-17) showed similar antibacterial action. Derivatives 47g, 48a, 48b, and 49 showed considerable potency with MICs of 16 to 64µg/mL.
